topics.title: Brain Network Organization Across Time and Development
topics.description: These studies examine how functional and structural brain organization relate across lifespan, development, and different brain states. They emphasize individual variability, dynamic connectivity, and network-level patterns in the human brain.

topics.title: Alzheimer’s Continuum and Cognitive Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer’s disease and related neurodegenerative progression across the aging spectrum, including mild cognitive impairment, preclinical stages, and dementia. It emphasizes biomarkers such as amyloid, tau, and brain age in relation to cognitive decline and disease prediction.

topics.title: MRI Methods for Brain Disease Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of brain pathology, especially white matter changes, lesions, and disease-specific imaging in disorders such as multiple sclerosis, stroke, epilepsy, and traumatic brain injury. It also includes advanced MRI approaches and computational tools for quantification, segmentation, and normative modeling across field strengths.

topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Depression and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression-related psychopathology across development, including trauma, early life stress, suicidality, and mood disorder subtypes. It also highlights brain connectivity and cortical structure findings linked to vulnerability, sex differences, and inflammatory influences.

topics.title: Neonatal Brain Development and Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development in neonates and children, especially in the context of preterm birth, congenital heart disease, and hypoxic-ischemic injury. It links cortical structure and folding with normative modeling and later cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: Neural Representation of Memory and Attention
topics.description: This cluster centers on how working memory, attention, and episodic recall are represented in the human brain. It emphasizes fMRI-based investigation of neural dynamics in visual cortex, hippocampus, and related regions during encoding and retrieval.

topics.title: Language and Learning Development in the Brain
topics.description: This cluster centers on language, reading, speech, and mathematical learning differences across development, including dyslexia and dyscalculia. It also highlights brain-based studies of language processing, comprehension, attention, and cognitive flexibility in multilingual and developing populations.

topics.title: Neural Basis of Emotion and Anxiety Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation, anxiety, and social-emotional processes are represented in the brain, with emphasis on amygdala and prefrontal mechanisms. It also includes social cognition, empathy, and related neural correlates across affective states and regulatory strategies.

topics.title: Psychosis Spectrum Biomarkers and Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying biomarkers and brain imaging patterns associated with schizophrenia and related psychosis spectrum conditions. It emphasizes heterogeneity, early risk, and transdiagnostic approaches for clinical prediction across schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and related disorders.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Aging in Human Brain
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based assessment of cerebrovascular physiology and its coupling to brain function in humans. It emphasizes aging and metabolic disease contexts, especially healthy aging and type 2 diabetes.

topics.title: Post-lesion Recovery and Deficit Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurological disorders and their associated deficits, especially stroke, multiple sclerosis, and traumatic brain injury. It emphasizes recovery, motor and cognitive outcomes, and lesion-based network mapping to explain disability and predict prognosis.

keywords: bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
accelerate zero echo time functional mri
topics.title: Advanced Neuroimaging Harmonization and High-Field fMRI
topics.description: The cluster centers on high-resolution and high-field neuroimaging methods, especially fMRI acquisition and analysis approaches. It also emphasizes denoising, harmonization, and benchmarking across sites and scanners.

topics.title: Epilepsy Connectivity and Seizure Localization
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy, especially drug resistant and focal forms, with emphasis on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones using SEEG and connectivity-based analyses. It also includes studies of altered signal propagation, cortico hippocampal dynamics, and related clinical features in pediatric and temporal lobe epilepsy.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Disorders and Neuromodulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease, related tremor and REM sleep behavior disorder phenotypes, and their progression to broader clinical manifestations. It also highlights deep brain stimulation and thalamotomy-related approaches in the context of Parkinsonian circuitry and pathology.

topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Differences in Autism and ADHD
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and ADHD across childhood and youth, emphasizing symptoms, traits, attention problems, and developmental variation. It also highlights associated brain abnormalities, connectivity changes, and brain-behavior relationships.

topics.title: Neurocircuitry of craving and addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related processes including craving, reward, and inhibitory control across substance use disorders. It also includes studies of drug cue reactivity and adolescent brain development, with some work involving psilocybin and ketamine challenges.

topics.title: Neuromodulation for Depression and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial stimulation approaches, especially TMS and iTBS, applied to depression, treatment-resistant depression, stroke, and mild cognitive impairment. The abstracts also emphasize connectivity, feasibility, efficacy, and individualized neuromodulation targeting.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Data Infrastructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on open science infrastructure and standards for neuroimaging, especially BIDS-based data organization, dataset handling, and tool integration. It emphasizes collaborative platforms and consortia that support clinical and research neuroimaging workflows.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
topics.title: Motor Representation and Rehabilitation Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on motor and body representation in the human motor system, especially action observation, imagery, and finger representations in primary motor cortex. It also includes stroke rehabilitation and VR-based interventions that modulate sensorimotor network activation and reorganization.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Chronic Pain Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain conditions and their links to pain perception, central sensitization, and related affective symptoms such as depression. It also includes disorder-specific syndromes like fibromyalgia, trigeminal neuralgia, and chronic migraine, with interest in how brain mechanisms relate to pain and analgesia.

keywords: brain foundation models a pilot study
cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Attention and Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural mechanisms underlying attention, cognition, and mental imagery, with links to behavioral responses and brain functionality. It also includes work on brain foundation models, cortical depth profiles, and cross-subject prediction for understanding and decoding neural organization.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Sleep Dynamics, Aging, and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: The cluster centers on human sleep architecture and state transitions, with emphasis on EEG features, arousal dynamics, and functional connectivity. It also links sleep to aging, consciousness, and glymphatic/cerebrospinal fluid-related physiology.

topics.title: OCD, Connectivity, and Therapy Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder and related anxiety/compulsivity phenotypes, with emphasis on frontostriatal and CSTC network alterations. It also includes treatment-related work on CBT and TMS outcomes and clinical response.

topics.title: Brain Development, Environment, and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on how early-life and environmental factors relate to adolescent and developmental brain morphology, cognition, and brain-behavior associations. It also includes work on brain aging, multimodal brain measures, and gene-environment interactions shaping human brain health.

topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Drivers of Eating Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on obesity and eating-disorder-related alterations in food choice, appetite regulation, and executive control. It also includes neural connectivity and hypothalamic network findings that may help identify candidate biomarkers and mechanisms underlying disordered eating.

keywords: cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
music and emotion in functional connectivity
audio visual reactivations facilitate musical skill retention
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Music Reward and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music listening, participation, and training shape reward, emotion, memory, and skill retention. It also highlights neural mechanisms and synchronization underlying musical processing and learning.

keywords: neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Risk, Decision Making, and Reward Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on how risk, value computation, and reward learning shape decision making across contexts such as trust, fairness, and real-world risky behavior. It also includes computational and neural accounts of choice history, exploration-exploitation, and optimal risk taking.

keywords: menopause specific hippocampal subfield volumes and memory at midlife a period of change
topics.title: Hormonal Effects on Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how hormonal changes across adolescence, the menstrual cycle, and menopause relate to brain structure, connectivity, and cognitive outcomes. It also includes work on sex- and puberty-related developmental trajectories and their links to risk and memory.

keywords: brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Neuroimaging and Glaucoma Phenotypes
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and functional brain phenotypes linked to glaucoma-related retinal measures, including neuroimaging, connectivity, and metabolic profiles. It also connects these findings to broader brain organization and disease-associated phenotypes.

keywords: csf cytokine patterns mirror striatal atrophy
white matter microstructure peripheral inflammatory cytokine level
topics.title: Neuroinflammation and Brain Network Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on relationships between inflammatory markers, immune signaling, and brain structure or connectivity across psychiatric and neurodegenerative conditions. It also includes work on CSF cytokine patterns, white matter microstructure, glymphatic function, and functional network responses.

keywords: the motion sick brain spectral changes
performance dependent parietal brain network reorganization
topics.title: Motion Sickness and Parietal Brain Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on motion sickness and related spectral and network changes in the brain, including evidence from electroencephalography and parietal cortex reorganization. It also includes spaceflight and rotating chair studies that probe underlying pathophysiological mechanisms.
